methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - VCU News
signals.signal_reading: VCU's partnership with AI Ready RVA signals deliberate community-level AI education infrastructure that could distribute capability beyond elite institutions, testing whether Richmond can anchor peer-to-peer fluency networks rather than just concentrate expertise.

signals.title: AI pressure grows on Richmond's young workforce - Axios
signals.signal_reading: Richmond's young workforce feeling AI pressure indicates capability demands are concentrating in metro markets where adoption races ahead of distributed skill-building, creating urgency gaps that favor those already in tech hubs.

signals.title: AI is here in Hampton Roads, and businesses and organizations are embracing it - The Virginian-Pilot
signals.signal_reading: Hampton Roads businesses embracing AI through platform adoption suggests shallow but distributed deployment—organizations accessing AI capability via software tools rather than developing deep technical fluency internally.

signals.title: EPA grant backs community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia - Virginia Tech News
signals.signal_reading: EPA-backed workforce training in Southwest Virginia creates potential for distributed skill development in a traditionally underserved region, testing whether community-driven models can bypass the concentration trap despite geographic disadvantages.

signals.title: Spanberger to consider legislation requiring state guidance on AI in schools - VPM
signals.signal_reading: State guidance on AI in schools would standardize platform-mediated access across Virginia regions, potentially enabling broad but shallow adoption rather than deep capability—distribution through policy rather than grassroots fluency.
